context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking across all LLM providers to monitor costs for each query by instrumenting provider calls and storing usage data in Answer metadata.
key_requirements: Track token counts for chat, embedding, rerank, and vision operations across all LLM providers
Store token usage data in Answer.metadata for programmatic access
Integrate with existing instrumentation system using BenchmarkHook pattern
constraints: Must use existing InstrumentedProxy system for transparent tracking without changing business logic
Token usage must be tracked at query level and embedded in Answer objects
Implementation must work across all provider types: OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama
stakeholders: user wanting to see LLM token costs per query
system integrator needing structured logging of usage data

in_scope: token counting for chat, embedding, rerank, vision operations across providers
tracking at query level in Answer metadata
integration through provider layer with instrumentation system
storing token usage as part of Answer.metadata
out_of_scope: implementation of custom tokenization logic per provider
changes to existing business logic beyond instrumentation
enhanced analytics or reporting features beyond basic tracking

existing_files: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients.
fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance.
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/token_counter.py — Main token counting logic with provider-specific implementations
fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py — Token usage data models and tracking utilities
fitz_ai/llm/providers/instrumented_providers.py — Instrumentation wrapper around all LLM providers
fitz_ai/llm/token_utils.py — Helper functions for token counting
fitz_ai/core/tracking_context.py — Context management for tracking scopes

assumptions.assumption: Use library-based token counting (e.g., tiktoken for OpenAI, anthropic-tokenizer for Anthropic) that's already available or can be added
assumptions.impact: Would require implementing custom tokenization logic per provider which is complex and error-prone

assumptions.assumption: Track at query level in Answer metadata, potentially also log via structured logging
assumptions.impact: Could lose tracking or make it hard to access for users

assumptions.assumption: Per-operation token counts (chat tokens, embedding tokens) rather than full breakdowns per message
assumptions.impact: Might miss optimization opportunities or cost drivers

assumptions.assumption: Integration through the provider layer in `fitz_ai/llm/providers` with instrumentation
assumptions.impact: Would require changing every LLM call site throughout codebase

assumptions.assumption: Usage should be available in `Answer.metadata`, potentially also via logging or observability APIs
assumptions.impact: Users won't know how to access the tracking information

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Tracking
approaches.description: Leverage existing InstrumentedProxy mechanism to wrap LLM provider methods and capture token usage from API responses.
pros: No breaking changes to public APIs
cons: Requires modification of provider classes to expose metadata
Token parsing logic must be implemented per-provider
best_for: Production systems with existing instrumentation patterns

approaches.name: Provider-Level Wrapper Enhancement
approaches.description: Create enhanced wrapper classes around each LLM provider that capture and aggregate token usage internally.
cons: Requires creating new wrapper classes or modifying existing ones
More code to maintain and test
best_for: Systems requiring robust, future-proof token tracking

architecture.reasoning: This approach is right because it leverages the existing InstrumentedProxy system already present in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py and avoids over-engineering by not modifying core interfaces or adding new API response fields that don't exist. The other approach, Provider-Level Wrapper Enhancement, is wrong because while it would work, it requires more extensive changes to provider classes which could introduce bugs and increase maintenance overhead unnecessarily when the instrumentation system already provides a clean way to intercept method calls.

key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_vs_modification: Use existing InstrumentedProxy instead of building new tracking system to reduce complexity and leverage current infra
key_tradeoffs.scope_limitation: Avoid modifying API schemas or SDK signatures to maintain backward compatibility and focus on internal tracking only

technology_considerations: InstrumentedProxy from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for method wrapping
OpenAI response usage metadata (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens) for token counting
LLM provider-specific APIs (Cohere/Anthropic/Ollama) for extracting token usage fields
architecture.scope_statement: Implement token usage tracking by instrumenting LLM provider methods with minimal changes to existing code paths and leveraging the current instrumentation framework for capturing prompt/completion token counts from external API responses.

adrs.title: ADR: Instrument LLM Providers for Token Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The system needs to track LLM token usage per query to provide cost visibility and optimization insights.
adrs.decision: Leverage the existing instrumentation infrastructure in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py to wrap core LLM provider methods without modifying public APIs or response schemas.
adrs.rationale: This approach minimizes architectural changes, avoids over-engineering by not redefining API responses, and uses already-existing mechanisms. It preserves backward compatibility while enabling token tracking at the provider level where raw usage data is available (e.g., OpenAI's response.usage).
consequences: Minimal code modifications required - only patch or wrap LLM provider methods in specific implementations like OpenAIChat, CohereChat, etc.
Token usage becomes available internally during query processing but doesn't change SDK/API signatures unless explicitly added to Answer class.
No breaking changes to existing interfaces; new functionality is opt-in via instrumentation hooks or debug utilities.
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 – Add token_usage fields to QueryResponse schema: rejected because the QueryResponse model does not exist in provided codebase and would require extensive API rework.

Alternative 2 – Modify SDK method signatures to return usage data directly: rejected because it breaks backward compatibility and adds unnecessary complexity without clear benefit for current use cases.
Alternative 3 – Create a global token counter registry: rejected because it introduces tight coupling and doesn't leverage existing structured logging or instrumentation patterns.

adrs.title: ADR: Data Sensitivity in Instrumentation-Based Tracking
adrs.context: The instrumentation-based approach uses InstrumentedProxy to track calls across OpenAIChat, OpenAIEmbedding, and FitzKragEngine components. The proxy captures request/response data for observability but doesn't specify what sensitive information might be included.
adrs.decision: InstrumentedProxy will capture all request/response data without filtering or sanitization to maintain complete traceability
adrs.rationale: This aligns with the existing instrumentation system's design and provides maximum debugging capability. The assumption is that all components are trusted and any security concerns are handled at higher layers.
consequences: Potential exposure of sensitive user data in logs/traces if not properly sanitized by downstream consumers
Increased data transfer overhead as complete payloads are captured instead of minimal necessary information
Requires additional documentation and training for developers on handling sensitive data in instrumented traces
alternatives_considered: Implement field-level filtering to exclude sensitive data (like API keys, user PII) before instrumentation
Add configuration options per component to enable/disable specific data capture levels
Create separate instrumentation channels for different sensitivity tiers of data

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wraps target objects to instrument method calls for benchmarking and caching hooks
responsibilities: Wrap methods of a target object with instrumentation
Track method calls using registered hooks
Provide transparent proxy behavior
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: OpenAIChat
components.purpose: Provides OpenAI chat completions with token tracking capability
responsibilities: Execute chat requests to OpenAI models
Capture and expose token usage from API responses
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, base_url: str | None, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

components.name: OpenAIEmbedding
components.purpose: Provides OpenAI embeddings with token tracking capability
responsibilities: Generate embeddings for text using OpenAI models
Capture and expose token usage from API responses
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, dimensions: int | None, base_url: str | None) -> None

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Knowledge Routing Augmented Generation engine for query processing and answer generation
responsibilities: Process queries through ingestion pipeline
Route queries to appropriate retrieval strategies
Generate final answers with aggregated token usage
interfaces: point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any

integration_points: LLM providers — wrap chat(), embed(), describe_image() methods with InstrumentedProxy to track token usage
FitzKragEngine — instrument core query processing steps that invoke LLMs (chat, embedding, reranking)
Answer construction — aggregate and attach total token counts when building final Answer objects

artifacts.content:
class BenchmarkHook(Protocol):
  def on_call_start(self, layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any: ...
  def on_call_end(self, context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None: ...
class CachingHook(Protocol):
  def get_cached_result(self, layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any: ...
  def cache_result(self, layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict, result: Any) -> None: ...
def register_hook(hook: BenchmarkHook) -> None: ...
def unregister_hook(hook: BenchmarkHook) -> None: ...
def clear_hooks() -> None: ...
def has_hooks() -> bool: ...
def get_hooks() -> list[BenchmarkHook]: ...
class InstrumentedProxy:
  def __init__(self, target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None): ...
  def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any: ...
  def _wrap_method(self, method: Callable, method_name: str) -> Callable: ...
  def __repr__(self) -> str: ...
  def __str__(self) -> str: ...
def maybe_wrap(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None) -> Any: ...
def wrap(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None) -> InstrumentedProxy: ...
artifacts.purpose: Provides instrumentation system for tracking LLM token usage and other performance metrics
Defines base protocols for LLM providers that will be instrumented for token tracking

artifacts.content:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any

class Answer:
  def __post_init__(self): ...
artifacts.purpose: Defines the answer structure that will be extended to include token usage metadata

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Provider Protocol Definition
phases.objective: Define standardized protocols for LLM providers including chat, embedding, reranking, and vision capabilities.
key_risks: Difficulty maintaining protocol compatibility with evolving LLM APIs

phases.name: OpenAI Provider Implementation
phases.objective: Implement OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK for chat, embedding, and vision services.
key_risks: Rate limiting and API key management

phases.name: Anthropic Provider Implementation
phases.objective: Implement Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK for chat and vision services.

phases.name: Cohere Provider Implementation
phases.objective: Implement Cohere provider wrappers for chat, embedding, and reranking services.

phases.name: Ollama Provider Implementation
phases.objective: Implement Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls for chat, embedding, reranking, and vision services.
key_risks: HTTP client reliability and connection pooling
Model compatibility across different Ollama versions

phases.name: Instrumentation System Integration
phases.objective: Integrate the instrumentation system to benchmark and cache LLM provider performance.
deliverables: Benchmark hooks for all provider classes

risks.description: LLM provider API outages or rate limiting could cause query failures or degraded performance
risks.mitigation: Implement circuit breaker patterns and fallback mechanisms for each LLM provider
risks.contingency: Switch to alternative LLM provider tier or fall back to cached responses when API calls fail
risks.verification: Assert that circuit breakers are triggered on repeated failures

risks.description: Inconsistent embedding dimensions across different providers could cause vector storage misalignment
risks.mitigation: Validate and normalize embedding dimensions at startup for all configured providers
risks.contingency: Fail gracefully with clear error message when dimension mismatch is detected during indexing
risks.verification: Assert that EmbeddingProvider.dimensions() returns consistent values across implementations

risks.description: LLM provider authentication tokens may be exposed or compromised in logs or error messages
risks.mitigation: Sanitize all logging and error outputs to remove sensitive auth headers before writing to logs
risks.contingency: Revoke compromised credentials immediately and rotate all affected tokens across providers
risks.verification: Assert that get_headers() implementations do not include raw secrets in output

risks.description: High concurrent load on LLM providers may trigger usage limits or billing overages
risks.mitigation: Implement request queuing and rate limiting with backpressure handling in the client layer
risks.contingency: Scale to additional provider accounts or tiers when usage thresholds are approached
risks.verification: Assert that LLM requests are properly throttled based on configured limits

risks.description: Different providers may produce varying quality outputs due to model differences, leading to inconsistent user experience
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific prompt engineering and output validation for each LLM tier
risks.contingency: Use a weighted voting system across multiple providers when available or default to most reliable tier
risks.verification: Assert that answer quality metrics are consistent across provider tiers

recommended_contingencies: Implement circuit breaker patterns and fallback mechanisms for each LLM provider
Fail gracefully with clear error message when dimension mismatch is detected during indexing
Revoke compromised credentials immediately and rotate all affected tokens across providers
Scale to additional provider accounts or tiers when usage thresholds are approached
Use a weighted voting system across multiple providers when available or default to most reliable tier
